definitions: A diagnostic tool used in Kubernetes to determine if a container is ready to accept traffic.
A mechanism to check if an application inside a container is fully started and capable of handling requests.
examples: The Readiness Probe failed, indicating the service was not yet available.
Configuring a Readiness Probe is crucial for zero-downtime deployments.
The Kubernetes scheduler uses the Readiness Probe to decide when to send traffic.
